   - Ads scaled slightly to S2 content $3,000/day only.
 - Watch next week: conversion at A $59, issue backlog age, impact of larger quotas on usage/compute, capacity tier 2 headroom, and whether cancellations stay low.
+
+## Week 8 / Day 49
+
+- Cash: $314,435. Subscribers: 3,764. Week 7 added 1,497 subscribers from 2,550 leads, with 8 cancellations and no overload/outage.
+- New S2 conversion at A $59 remained excellent: content S2 produced 2,353 leads from $21,000 and 1,390 paid S2 content subscribers. First-month revenue plus early renewals were $75,247, almost covering total weekly operating costs.
+- Active mix: S2 A 3,627, S2 B 59, S2 C 1; S1/S3 small and not worth paid acquisition right now.
+- Issues: 315 open. S2 has 308 open, all fresh; S1 has 7 open including one 10-day issue. Support spend resolves many same week but must scale.
+- Usage: 1.83M/week; capacity tier 2 has room, but higher quotas and growth will increase compute.
+- Changes:
+  - New prices: A $69, B $179, C $299. S2 lead promo stays $10, so new S2 A cash is $59.
+  - Quotas raised again: A 2000, B 8000, C 20000.
+  - Dev cut to maintenance: $1,300/day total (global $200, S1 $100, S2 $900, S3 $100).
+  - Ops increased to $9,200/day total: global $3,000, S1 $1,000, S2 $5,000, S3 $200.
+  - Ads scaled to S2 content $5,000/day.
+  - No social post; let operational changes prove themselves.
+- Watch next week: A $69 conversion, support backlog age, compute spend under higher quotas, tier 2 capacity headroom, and whether renewals begin lifting cash.
